content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 32 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 116r–118r (LC PDF pp. 230–234) | **Source Images:** DEU_032_page_001.jpg, DEU_032_page_002.jpg, DEU_032_page_003.jpg, DEU_032_page_004.jpg, DEU_032_page_005.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 163–165 (pa_0163–pa_0165)

*Note: The Ha'azinu is set in two-column poetic half-lines (hemistichs) in the Leningrad Codex. This layout is preserved below, with each hemistich on its own half-line, separated by a space, following the manuscript's bicolon format.*

---

**1**
Give ear, O heavens, and I will speak,        and let the earth hear the utterances of my mouth.
**2**
Let my teaching drop as the rain,              let my speech drip as the dew,
as showers upon the tender grass              and as heavy drops upon the herbage.
**3**
For I will proclaim the name of the LORD;    ascribe greatness to our God!
**4**
The Rock [ha-tsur] — his work is perfect,    for all his ways are justice;
a God of faithfulness, without iniquity,       righteous and upright is he.
**5**
Corruption is his — not his [children's];       a generation crooked and perverse.
**6**
Is this how you requite the LORD,            O foolish people and unwise?
Is he not your Father who acquired you?    He made you and established you.
**7**
Remember the days of old;                      consider the years of many generations.
Ask your father and he will declare to you,   your elders and they will tell you.
**8**
When the Most High gave an inheritance to the nations,
                                                           when he separated the sons of Adam,
he set the borders of the peoples             according to the number of the sons of Yisra'el.
**9**
For the LORD's portion is his people;          Ya'akov is the measure of his inheritance.
**10**
He found him in a wilderness land,            and in a waste — a howling wasteland;
he encompassed him, he cared for him,      he guarded him as the pupil of his eye.
**11**
As an eagle stirs up its nest,                   flutters over its young,
spreads its wings, takes them,               carries them on its pinions —
**12**
The LORD alone led him,                         and no foreign god was with him.
**13**
He made him ride on the heights of the land and he fed him the produce of the field,
and he suckled him with honey from the rock  and oil from the flint of the crag,
**14**
Curds of cattle and milk of flocks,             together with fat of lambs,
and rams — sons of Bashan — and goats,   with the fat of kidneys of wheat;
and you drank the blood of the grape as foaming wine.
**15**
And Yeshurun grew fat and kicked —         you grew fat, thick, and sleek —
and he forsook God who made him           and he spurned the Rock of his salvation.
**16**
They stirred his jealousy with foreign [gods],  with detestable things [to'evot] they provoked him.
**17**
They slaughtered to demons — not God,     to gods whom they had not known,
new [gods] that had come from near,         your fathers had not dreaded them.
**18**
The Rock who fathered you — you forgot,    and you neglected God who birthed you.
**19**
And the LORD saw and rejected [them]        in the provocation of his sons and his daughters.
**20**
And he said: "I will hide my face from them;   I will see what their end will be,
for they are a generation of reversals —      children in whom there is no faithfulness.
**21**
They stirred my jealousy with a not-god,     they provoked me with their vanities [havaleihem];
and I will stir their jealousy with a not-people,  with a foolish nation I will provoke them.
**22**
For a fire has been kindled in my anger       and it burns to the lowest Sheol,
and it devours the earth and its produce      and sets ablaze the foundations of the mountains.
**23**
I will heap evils upon them;                      my arrows I will spend upon them.
**24**
Wasting of hunger and consumed by burning [fever]  and bitter plague;
and the tooth of beasts I will send against them,    with venom of crawlers in the dust.
**25**
Outside, the sword bereaves,                    and inside, terror —
both young man and young woman,           the suckling with the gray-haired man.
**26**
I said I would disperse them;                    I would make their remembrance cease from among men,
**27**
were it not that the anger of the enemy I dreaded,
                                                           lest their adversaries misconstrue,
lest they say: 'Our hand has triumphed       and the LORD has not done all this.'
**28**
For they are a nation void of counsel,         and there is no understanding in them.
**29**
If only they were wise they would perceive this,   they would understand their latter end.
**30**
How could one chase a thousand             and two put ten thousand to flight,
unless their Rock had sold them              and the LORD had delivered them up?
**31**
For their rock is not as our Rock —           even our enemies themselves [are the] judges.
**32**
For their vine is from the vine of Sodom      and from the terraces of Gomorrah;
their grapes are grapes of poison,            bitter clusters are theirs.
**33**
The venom of serpents is their wine,          and the cruel poison of asps.
**34**
Is this not stored up with me,                   sealed in my treasuries?
**35**
Mine is vengeance and recompense,          for the time when their foot slips;
for near is the day of their calamity          and the things prepared hasten for them.
**36**
For the LORD will judge his people             and over his servants he will relent;
for he will see that the hand has gone        and nothing is left, restrained or free.
**37**
And he will say: "Where are their gods,        the rock in which they sought refuge,
**38**
who ate the fat of their sacrifices             and drank the wine of their libations?
Let them rise and help you,                     let them be a hiding place for you!
**39**
See now that I, I am he,                          and there is no god with me;
I kill and I make alive;                             I wound and I myself heal;
and there is no one who delivers from my hand.
**40**
For I lift my hand to the heavens              and say: As I live forever,
**41**
if I whet my flashing sword                      and my hand seizes judgment in justice,
I will return vengeance to my adversaries   and to those who hate me I will repay.
**42**
I will make my arrows drunk with blood       and my sword will devour flesh —
from the blood of the slain and the captive,  from the long-haired head of the enemy."
**43**
Praise his people, O nations,                    for the blood of his servants he will avenge,
and vengeance he will return to his adversaries   and he will atone for his land — his people.

**44** And Moshe came and spoke all the words of this song in the ears of the people, he and Hoshea son of Nun. **45** And Moshe finished speaking all these words to all Yisra'el. **46** And he said to them: "Set your heart to all the words with which I am testifying among you today, which you shall command your children to take care to do, all the words of this instruction [torah]. **47** For it is not an empty word for you, for it is your life — and by this word you will lengthen days upon the land which you are crossing the Yarden there to possess it."

**48** And the LORD spoke to Moshe on that very same day, saying: **49** "Go up to this mountain of Avarim, Mount Nevo, which is in the land of Moav, which is opposite Yericho, and see the land of Kena'an which I am giving to the sons of Yisra'el as a possession. **50** And die on the mountain which you are going up there, and be gathered to your kinspeople, as Aharon your brother died on Mount Hor and was gathered to his kinspeople, **51** because you acted unfaithfully against me in the midst of the sons of Yisra'el at the waters of Merivat-Kadesh in the wilderness of Tzin, because you did not sanctify me in the midst of the sons of Yisra'el. **52** For you shall see the land from opposite, but you shall not go there — into the land which I am giving to the sons of Yisra'el."

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**Poetic Layout:** The Leningrad Codex sets Ha'azinu in a distinctive two-column format across five manuscript pages (LC pp.230–234), with each verse divided into two half-lines written in parallel columns (bicolon hemistichs). This is the most elaborate manuscript treatment given to any poem in the Torah. EMET has preserved this layout using indented parallel half-lines.

**V.5 — "Corruption is his — not his":** שִׁחֵת לוֹ לֹא בָּנָיו (shichet lo lo vanav) — among the most difficult phrases in the entire MT. The text is syntactically abrupt. The LXX Vaticanus (pa_0163) reads ἡμάρτοσαν οὐκ αὐτῷ τέκνα μωμητά ("they sinned against him — children not his, blemished ones"), reflecting a different reading and smoother syntax. EMET preserves the MT as it stands, which attributes the corruption to "him" (i.e., Yisra'el has corrupted what belongs to God) while "not his children" expresses their alienation. The textual crux is well-attested.

**V.8 — "sons of Yisra'el":** The MT reads בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל (bney yisra'el). The LXX reads ἀγγέλων θεοῦ ("angels of God"), reflecting a Vorlage reading בְּנֵי אֵל or בְּנֵי אֱלֹהִים ("sons of God"), also attested in the Dead Sea Scrolls (4QDeut^j). This is a major textual variant with significant theological implications: the LXX/DSS version suggests the nations were parceled out to divine beings, while the MT reading centers the division on Yisra'el's number. EMET follows the LC MT here.

**V.15 — Yeshurun:** יְשֻׁרוּן — a poetic name for Yisra'el, from the root y-sh-r ("upright/straight"). The LXX renders Ισραηλ, losing the wordplay. EMET preserves the Hebrew name.

**V.17 — "demons":** שֵׁדִים (shedim), also in Ps 106:37. The LXX renders δαιμονίοις ("demons"), confirming the LC.

**V.21 — "not-people":** לֹא-עַם (lo-am) — the exact reversal of the covenant formula. This anticipates the Hosea tradition. The LXX renders ἐπ᾽ οὐκ ἔθνει, parallel.

**V.32 — "terraces":** שַׂדְמֹת (sadmot) is a hapax legomenon; the LXX renders ἄμπελος Σοδόμων ("vine of Sodom") without rendering sadmot specifically. "Terraces" or "fields/slopes" is the most likely lexical equivalent in context.

**V.35 — "Mine is vengeance":** לִי נָקָם (li naqam). The MT is unambiguous. Paul quotes this in Romans 12:19 and the epistle to the Hebrews (10:30) in agreement with the LXX form ἐν ἡμέρᾳ ἐκδικήσεως. The Targums, DSS, and LXX all show some variation in this verse.

**V.43 — LXX expansion:** The LXX Vaticanus contains a significantly expanded version of v.43 with additional cola: εὐφράνθητε, οὐρανοί, ἅμα αὐτῷ / καὶ προσκυνησάτωσαν αὐτῷ πάντες υἱοὶ θεοῦ ("rejoice, O heavens, together with him, and let all sons of God bow to him"). This expanded form is also attested in 4QDeut^q from Qumran and is quoted in Hebrews 1:6. The MT is shorter. EMET translates the MT. The LXX expansion is noted for comparison.

**Vv.44 — "Hoshea":** The MT names Yehoshua as הוֹשֵׁעַ (Hoshea) here — his earlier name before Moshe renamed him (Num 13:16). The LXX renders Ἰησοῦς throughout. EMET follows the MT's deliberate use of the earlier name at this point.

**Vv.48–52 — Narrative frame:** These verses interrupt before ch.33, commanding Moshe to ascend Nevo and die. They parallel Num 27:12–14 almost verbatim. The LXX (pa_0165) follows the MT closely here.

— *EMET*
